title: Tender Crunchy Egyptian Bamboo Shoot Tagine
description: A comforting and pungent Egyptian dish featuring bamboo shoots, poached to perfection for a delicate crunch. Serve with naan or pita bread for a satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Bamboo shoots (2 cups, trimmed and sliced)
ingredients.notes: Look for shoots with no signs of softening or mold.

instructions: Heat the oil in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the cumin, cinnamon, turmeric, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the bamboo shoots and cook until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the stock and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the shoots are tender, about 20-25 minutes.
Stir in the fresh parsley and cook for 1 minute.
Serve hot, garnished with lemon wedges.
This dish can be made ahead and refrigerated or frozen for later use.
Serve with naan or pita bread, if desired.
